net.LUK_12_20: But God said to him, ‘You fool! This very night your life will be demanded back from you, but who will get what you have prepared for yourself?’
net.LUK_12_21: So it is with the one who stores up riches for himself, but is not rich toward God.” Exhortation Not to Worry
net.LUK_12_22: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
net.LUK_12_23: For there is more to life than food, and more to the body than clothing.
net.LUK_12_24: Consider the ravens: They do not sow or reap, they have no storeroom or barn, yet God feeds them. How much more valuable are you than the birds!
net.LUK_12_25: And which of you by worrying can add an hour to his life?
net.LUK_12_26: So if you cannot do such a very little thing as this, why do you worry about the rest?
net.LUK_12_27: Consider how the flowers grow; they do not work or spin. Yet I tell you, not even Solomon in all his glory was clothed like one of these!
net.LUK_12_28: And if this is how God clothes the wild grass, which is here today and tomorrow is tossed into the fire to heat the oven, how much more will he clothe you, you people of little faith!
net.LUK_12_29: So do not be overly concerned about what you will eat and what you will drink, and do not worry about such things.
net.LUK_12_30: For all the nations of the world pursue these things, and your Father knows that you need them.
net.LUK_12_31: Instead, pursue his kingdom, and these things will be given to you as well.
net.LUK_12_32: “Do not be afraid, little flock, for your Father is well pleased to give you the kingdom.
net.LUK_12_33: Sell your possessions and give to the poor. Provide yourselves purses that do not wear out—a treasure in heaven that never decreases, where no thief approaches and no moth destroys.
net.LUK_12_34: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also. Call to Faithful Stewardship
net.LUK_12_35: “Get dressed for service and keep your lamps burning;
net.LUK_12_36: be like people waiting for their master to come back from the wedding celebration, so that when he comes and knocks they can immediately open the door for him.
net.LUK_12_37: Blessed are those slaves whom their master finds alert when he returns! I tell you the truth, he will dress himself to serve, have them take their place at the table, and will come and wait on them!
net.LUK_12_38: Even if he comes in the second or third watch of the night and finds them alert, blessed are those slaves!
net.LUK_12_39: But understand this: If the owner of the house had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have let his house be broken into.
net.LUK_12_40: You also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour when you do not expect him.”
net.LUK_12_41: Then Peter said, “Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone?”
net.LUK_12_42: The Lord replied, “Who then is the faithful and wise manager, whom the master puts in charge of his household servants, to give them their allowance of food at the proper time?
net.LUK_12_43: Blessed is that slave whom his master finds at work when he returns.
net.LUK_12_44: I tell you the truth, the master will put him in charge of all his possessions.
net.LUK_12_45: But if that slave should say to himself, ‘My master is delayed in returning,’ and he begins to beat the other slaves, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk,
net.LUK_12_46: then the master of that slave will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he does not foresee, and will cut him in two, and assign him a place with the unfaithful.
net.LUK_12_47: That servant who knew his master’s will but did not get ready or do what his master asked will receive a severe beating.
net.LUK_12_48: But the one who did not know his master’s will and did things worthy of punishment will receive a light beating. From everyone who has been given much, much will be required, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked. Not Peace, but Division
net.LUK_12_49: “I have come to bring fire on the earth—and how I wish it were already kindled!
net.LUK_12_50: I have a baptism to undergo, and how distressed I am until it is finished!
net.LUK_12_51: Do you think I have come to bring peace on earth? No, I tell you, but rather division!
net.LUK_12_52: For from now on there will be five in one household divided, three against two and two against three.
net.LUK_12_53: They will be divided, father against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.” Reading the Signs
net.LUK_12_54: Jesus also said to the crowds, “When you see a cloud rising in the west, you say at once, ‘A rainstorm is coming,’ and it does.
net.LUK_12_55: And when you see the south wind blowing, you say, ‘There will be scorching heat,’ and there is.
net.LUK_12_56: You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky, but how can you not know how to interpret the present time? Clear the Debts
net.LUK_12_57: “And why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?
net.LUK_12_58: As you are going with your accuser before the magistrate, make an effort to settle with him on the way, so that he will not drag you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
net.LUK_12_59: I tell you, you will never get out of there until you have paid the very last cent!”
net.LUK_13_1: Now there were some present on that occasion who told him about the Galileans whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices.
net.LUK_13_2: He answered them, “Do you think these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans, because they suffered these things?
net.LUK_13_3: No, I tell you! But unless you repent, you will all perish as well!
net.LUK_13_4: Or those eighteen who were killed when the tower in Siloam fell on them, do you think they were worse offenders than all the others who live in Jerusalem?
net.LUK_13_5: No, I tell you! But unless you repent you will all perish as well!” Warning to Israel to Bear Fruit
net.LUK_13_6: Then Jesus told this parable: “A man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard, and he came looking for fruit on it and found none.
net.LUK_13_7: So he said to the worker who tended the vineyard, ‘For three years now, I have come looking for fruit on this fig tree, and each time I inspect it I find none. Cut it down! Why should it continue to deplete the soil?’
net.LUK_13_8: But the worker answered him, ‘Sir, leave it alone this year too, until I dig around it and put fertilizer on it.
net.LUK_13_9: Then if it bears fruit next year, very well, but if not, you can cut it down.’” Healing on the Sabbath
net.LUK_13_10: Now he was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath,
net.LUK_13_11: and a woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and could not straighten herself up completely.
net.LUK_13_12: When Jesus saw her, he called her to him and said, “Woman, you are freed from your infirmity.”
net.LUK_13_13: Then he placed his hands on her, and immediately she straightened up and praised God.
net.LUK_13_14: But the president of the synagogue, indignant because Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, said to the crowd, “There are six days on which work should be done! So come and be healed on those days, and not on the Sabbath day.”
net.LUK_13_15: Then the Lord answered him, “You hypocrites! Does not each of you on the Sabbath untie his ox or his donkey from its stall, and lead it to water?
net.LUK_13_16: Then shouldn’t this woman, a daughter of Abraham whom Satan bound for eighteen long years, be released from this imprisonment on the Sabbath day?”
net.LUK_13_17: When he said this all his adversaries were humiliated, but the entire crowd was rejoicing at all the wonderful things he was doing. On the Kingdom of God
net.LUK_13_18: Thus Jesus asked, “What is the kingdom of God like? To what should I compare it?
net.LUK_13_19: It is like a mustard seed that a man took and sowed in his garden. It grew and became a tree, and the wild birds nested in its branches.”
net.LUK_13_20: Again he said, “To what should I compare the kingdom of God?
net.LUK_13_21: It is like yeast that a woman took and mixed with three measures of flour until all the dough had risen.” The Narrow Door
net.LUK_13_22: Then Jesus traveled throughout towns and villages, teaching and making his way toward Jerusalem.
net.LUK_13_23: Someone asked him, “Lord, will only a few be saved?” So he said to them,
net.LUK_13_24: “Exert every effort to enter through the narrow door, because many, I tell you, will try to enter and will not be able to.
net.LUK_13_25: Once the head of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and start to knock on the door and beg him, ‘Lord, let us in!’ But he will answer you, ‘I don’t know where you come from.’
net.LUK_13_26: Then you will begin to say, ‘We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.’
net.LUK_13_27: But he will reply, ‘I don’t know where you come from! Go away from me, all you evildoers!’
net.LUK_13_28: There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in the kingdom of God but you yourselves thrown out.
net.LUK_13_29: Then people will come from east and west, and from north and south, and take their places at the banquet table in the kingdom of God.
net.LUK_13_30: But indeed, some are last who will be first, and some are first who will be last.” Going to Jerusalem
net.LUK_13_31: At that time, some Pharisees came up and said to Jesus, “Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.”
net.LUK_13_32: But he said to them, “Go and tell that fox, ‘Look, I am casting out demons and performing healings today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
net.LUK_13_33: Nevertheless I must go on my way today and tomorrow and the next day, because it is impossible that a prophet should be killed outside Jerusalem.’
net.LUK_13_34: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who are sent to you! How often I have longed to gather your children together as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you would have none of it!
net.LUK_13_35: Look, your house is forsaken! And I tell you, you will not see me until you say, ‘Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord!’”
net.LUK_14_1: Now one Sabbath when Jesus went to dine at the house of a leader of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
net.LUK_14_2: There right in front of him was a man whose body was swollen with fluid.
net.LUK_14_3: So Jesus asked the experts in religious law and the Pharisees, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath or not?”
net.LUK_14_4: But they remained silent. So Jesus took hold of the man, healed him, and sent him away.
net.LUK_14_5: Then he said to them, “Which of you, if you have a son or an ox that has fallen into a well on a Sabbath day, will not immediately pull him out?”
net.LUK_14_6: But they could not reply to this. On Seeking Seats of Honor
net.LUK_14_7: Then when Jesus noticed how the guests chose the places of honor, he told them a parable. He said to them,
net.LUK_14_8: “When you are invited by someone to a wedding feast, do not take the place of honor, because a person more distinguished than you may have been invited by your host.
net.LUK_14_9: So the host who invited both of you will come and say to you, ‘Give this man your place.’ Then, ashamed, you will begin to move to the least important place.
net.LUK_14_10: But when you are invited, go and take the least important place, so that when your host approaches he will say to you, ‘Friend, move up here to a better place.’ Then you will be honored in the presence of all who share the meal with you.
net.LUK_14_11: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but the one who humbles himself will be exalted.”
net.LUK_14_12: He said also to the man who had invited him, “When you host a dinner or a banquet, don’t invite your friends or your brothers or your relatives or rich neighbors so you can be invited by them in return and get repaid.
net.LUK_14_13: But when you host an elaborate meal, invite the poor, the crippled, the lame, and the blind.
net.LUK_14_14: Then you will be blessed, because they cannot repay you, for you will be repaid at the resurrection of the righteous.” The Parable of the Great Banquet
net.LUK_14_15: When one of those at the meal with Jesus heard this, he said to him, “Blessed is everyone who will feast in the kingdom of God!”
net.LUK_14_16: But Jesus said to him, “A man once gave a great banquet and invited many guests.
net.LUK_14_17: At the time for the banquet he sent his slave to tell those who had been invited, ‘Come, because everything is now ready.’
net.LUK_14_18: But one after another they all began to make excuses. The first said to him, ‘I have bought a field, and I must go out and see it. Please excuse me.’
net.LUK_14_19: Another said, ‘I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I am going out to examine them. Please excuse me.’
net.LUK_14_20: Another said, ‘I just got married, and I cannot come.’
net.LUK_14_21: So the slave came back and reported this to his master. Then the master of the household was furious and said to his slave, ‘Go out quickly to the streets and alleys of the city, and bring in the poor, the crippled, the blind, and the lame.’
net.LUK_14_22: Then the slave said, ‘Sir, what you instructed has been done, and there is still room.’
net.LUK_14_23: So the master said to his slave, ‘Go out to the highways and country roads and urge people to come in, so that my house will be filled.
net.LUK_14_24: For I tell you, not one of those individuals who were invited will taste my banquet!’” Counting the Cost
net.LUK_14_25: Now large crowds were accompanying Jesus, and turning to them he said,
net.LUK_14_26: “If anyone comes to me and does not hate his own father and mother, and wife and children, and brothers and sisters, and even his own life, he cannot be my disciple.
net.LUK_14_27: Whoever does not carry his own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
net.LUK_14_28: For which of you, wanting to build a tower, doesn’t sit down first and compute the cost to see if he has enough money to complete it?
net.LUK_14_29: Otherwise, when he has laid a foundation and is not able to finish the tower, all who see it will begin to make fun of him.
net.LUK_14_30: They will say, ‘This man began to build and was not able to finish!’
net.LUK_14_31: Or what king, going out to confront another king in battle, will not sit down first and determine whether he is able with 10,000 to oppose the one coming against him with 20,000?
net.LUK_14_32: If he cannot succeed, he will send a representative while the other is still a long way off and ask for terms of peace.
net.LUK_14_33: In the same way therefore not one of you can be my disciple if he does not renounce all his own possessions.
net.LUK_14_34: “Salt is good, but if salt loses its flavor, how can its flavor be restored?
net.LUK_14_35: It is of no value for the soil or for the manure pile; it is to be thrown out. The one who has ears to hear had better listen!”
net.LUK_15_1: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming to hear him.
net.LUK_15_2: But the Pharisees and the experts in the law were complaining, “This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.”
net.LUK_15_3: So Jesus told them this parable:
net.LUK_15_4: “Which one of you, if he has a hundred sheep and loses one of them, would not leave the ninety-nine in the open pasture and go look for the one that is lost until he finds it?
net.LUK_15_5: Then when he has found it, he places it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
net.LUK_15_6: Returning home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, telling them, ‘Rejoice with me, because I have found my sheep that was lost.’
net.LUK_15_7: I tell you, in the same way there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to repent.
net.LUK_15_8: “Or what woman, if she has ten silver coins and loses one of them, does not light a lamp, sweep the house, and search thoroughly until she finds it?
net.LUK_15_9: Then when she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the coin that I had lost.’
net.LUK_15_10: In the same way, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of God’s angels over one sinner who repents.” The Parable of the Compassionate Father
net.LUK_15_11: Then Jesus said, “A man had two sons.
net.LUK_15_12: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the share of the estate that will belong to me.’ So he divided his assets between them.
net.LUK_15_13: After a few days, the younger son gathered together all he had and left on a journey to a distant country, and there he squandered his wealth with a wild lifestyle.
net.LUK_15_14: Then after he had spent everything, a severe famine took place in that country, and he began to be in need.
net.LUK_15_15: So he went and worked for one of the citizens of that country, who sent him to his fields to feed pigs.
net.LUK_15_16: He was longing to eat the carob pods the pigs were eating, but no one gave him anything.
net.LUK_15_17: But when he came to his senses he said, ‘How many of my father’s hired workers have food enough to spare, but here I am dying from hunger!
net.LUK_15_18: I will get up and go to my father and say to him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
net.LUK_15_19: I am no longer worthy to be called your son; treat me like one of your hired workers.”’
net.LUK_15_20: So he got up and went to his father. But while he was still a long way from home his father saw him, and his heart went out to him; he ran and hugged his son and kissed him.
net.LUK_15_21: Then his son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
net.LUK_15_22: But the father said to his slaves, ‘Hurry! Bring the best robe, and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!
net.LUK_15_23: Bring the fattened calf and kill it! Let us eat and celebrate,
net.LUK_15_24: because this son of mine was dead, and is alive again—he was lost and is found!’ So they began to celebrate.
net.LUK_15_25: “Now his older son was in the field. As he came and approached the house, he heard music and dancing.
net.LUK_15_26: So he called one of the slaves and asked what was happening.
net.LUK_15_27: The slave replied, ‘Your brother has returned, and your father has killed the fattened calf because he got his son back safe and sound.’
net.LUK_15_28: But the older son became angry and refused to go in. His father came out and appealed to him,
net.LUK_15_29: but he answered his father, ‘Look! These many years I have worked like a slave for you, and I never disobeyed your commands. Yet you never gave me even a goat so that I could celebrate with my friends!
net.LUK_15_30: But when this son of yours came back, who has devoured your assets with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him!’
net.LUK_15_31: Then the father said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and everything that belongs to me is yours.
net.LUK_15_32: It was appropriate to celebrate and be glad, for your brother was dead, and is alive; he was lost and is found.’”
net.LUK_16_1: Jesus also said to the disciples, “There was a rich man who was informed of accusations that his manager was wasting his assets.
net.LUK_16_2: So he called the manager in and said to him, ‘What is this I hear about you? Turn in the account of your administration, because you can no longer be my manager.’
net.LUK_16_3: Then the manager said to himself, ‘What should I do, since my master is taking my position away from me? I’m not strong enough to dig, and I’m too ashamed to beg.
net.LUK_16_4: I know what to do so that when I am put out of management, people will welcome me into their homes.’
net.LUK_16_5: So he contacted his master’s debtors one by one. He asked the first, ‘How much do you owe my master?’
net.LUK_16_6: The man replied, ‘100 measures of olive oil.’ The manager said to him, ‘Take your bill, sit down quickly, and write fifty.’
net.LUK_16_7: Then he said to another, ‘And how much do you owe?’ The second man replied, ‘100 measures of wheat.’ The manager said to him, ‘Take your bill, and write 80.’
net.LUK_16_8: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted shrewdly. For the people of this world are more shrewd in dealing with their contemporaries than the people of light.
net.LUK_16_9: And I tell you, make friends for yourselves by how you use worldly wealth, so that when it runs out you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
net.LUK_16_10: “The one who is faithful in a very little is also faithful in much, and the one who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much.
net.LUK_16_11: If then you haven’t been trustworthy in handling worldly wealth, who will entrust you with the true riches?
net.LUK_16_12: And if you haven’t been trustworthy with someone else’s property, who will give you your own ?
net.LUK_16_13: No servant can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and money.” More Warnings about the Pharisees
net.LUK_16_14: The Pharisees (who loved money) heard all this and ridiculed him.
net.LUK_16_15: But Jesus said to them, “You are the ones who justify yourselves in men’s eyes, but God knows your hearts. For what is highly prized among men is utterly detestable in God’s sight.
net.LUK_16_16: “The law and the prophets were in force until John; since then, the good news of the kingdom of God has been proclaimed, and everyone is urged to enter it.
net.LUK_16_17: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for one tiny stroke of a letter in the law to become void.
net.LUK_16_18: “Everyone who divorces his wife and marries someone else commits adultery, and the one who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery. The Rich Man and Lazarus
net.LUK_16_19: “There was a rich man who dressed in purple and fine linen and who feasted sumptuously every day.
net.LUK_16_20: But at his gate lay a poor man named Lazarus whose body was covered with sores,
net.LUK_16_21: who longed to eat what fell from the rich man’s table. In addition, the dogs came and licked his sores.
net.LUK_16_22: “Now the poor man died and was carried by the angels to Abraham’s side. The rich man also died and was buried.
net.LUK_16_23: And in Hades, as he was in torment, he looked up and saw Abraham far off with Lazarus at his side.
net.LUK_16_24: So he called out, ‘Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because I am in anguish in this fire.’
net.LUK_16_25: But Abraham said, ‘Child, remember that in your lifetime you received your good things and Lazarus likewise bad things, but now he is comforted here and you are in anguish.
net.LUK_16_26: Besides all this, a great chasm has been fixed between us, so that those who want to cross over from here to you cannot do so, and no one can cross from there to us.’
net.LUK_16_27: So the rich man said, ‘Then I beg you, father—send Lazarus to my father’s house
net.LUK_16_28: (for I have five brothers) to warn them so that they don’t come into this place of torment.’
net.LUK_16_29: But Abraham said, ‘They have Moses and the prophets; they must respond to them.’
net.LUK_16_30: Then the rich man said, ‘No, father Abraham, but if someone from the dead goes to them, they will repent.’
net.LUK_16_31: He replied to him, ‘If they do not respond to Moses and the prophets, they will not be convinced even if someone rises from the dead.’”
net.LUK_17_1: Jesus said to his disciples, “Stumbling blocks are sure to come, but woe to the one through whom they come!
net.LUK_17_2: It would be better for him to have a millstone tied around his neck and be thrown into the sea than for him to cause one of these little ones to sin.
net.LUK_17_3: Watch yourselves! If your brother sins, rebuke him. If he repents, forgive him.
net.LUK_17_4: Even if he sins against you seven times in a day, and seven times returns to you saying, ‘I repent,’ you must forgive him.”
net.LUK_17_5: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith!”
net.LUK_17_6: So the Lord replied, “If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this black mulberry tree, ‘Be pulled out by the roots and planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.
net.LUK_17_7: “Would any one of you say to your slave who comes in from the field after plowing or shepherding sheep, ‘Come at once and sit down for a meal’?
net.LUK_17_8: Won’t the master instead say to him, ‘Get my dinner ready, and make yourself ready to serve me while I eat and drink. Then you may eat and drink’?
net.LUK_17_9: He won’t thank the slave because he did what he was told, will he?
net.LUK_17_10: So you too, when you have done everything you were commanded to do, should say, ‘We are slaves undeserving of special praise; we have only done what was our duty.’” The Grateful Leper
net.LUK_17_11: Now on the way to Jerusalem, Jesus was passing along between Samaria and Galilee.
net.LUK_17_12: As he was entering a village, ten men with leprosy met him. They stood at a distance,
net.LUK_17_13: raised their voices and said, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us.”
net.LUK_17_14: When he saw them he said, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” And as they went along, they were cleansed.
net.LUK_17_15: Then one of them, when he saw he was healed, turned back, praising God with a loud voice.
net.LUK_17_16: He fell with his face to the ground at Jesus’ feet and thanked him. (Now he was a Samaritan.)
net.LUK_17_17: Then Jesus said, “Were not ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?
net.LUK_17_18: Was no one found to turn back and give praise to God except this foreigner?”
net.LUK_17_19: Then he said to the man, “Get up and go your way. Your faith has made you well.” The Coming of the Kingdom
net.LUK_17_20: Now at one point the Pharisees asked Jesus when the kingdom of God was coming, so he answered, “The kingdom of God is not coming with signs to be observed,
net.LUK_17_21: nor will they say, ‘Look, here it is!’ or ‘There!’ For indeed, the kingdom of God is in your midst.” The Coming of the Son of Man
net.LUK_17_22: Then he said to the disciples, “The days are coming when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
net.LUK_17_23: Then people will say to you, ‘Look, there he is!’ or ‘Look, here he is!’ Do not go out or chase after them.
net.LUK_17_24: For just like the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one side to the other, so will the Son of Man be in his day.
net.LUK_17_25: But first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
net.LUK_17_26: Just as it was in the days of Noah, so too it will be in the days of the Son of Man.
net.LUK_17_27: People were eating, they were drinking, they were marrying, they were being given in marriage—right up to the day Noah entered the ark. Then the flood came and destroyed them all.
net.LUK_17_28: Likewise, just as it was in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, building;
net.LUK_17_29: but on the day Lot went out from Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
net.LUK_17_30: It will be the same on the day the Son of Man is revealed.
net.LUK_17_31: On that day, anyone who is on the roof, with his goods in the house, must not come down to take them away, and likewise the person in the field must not turn back.
net.LUK_17_33: Whoever tries to keep his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life will preserve it.
net.LUK_17_34: I tell you, in that night there will be two people in one bed; one will be taken and the other left.
net.LUK_17_35: There will be two women grinding grain together; one will be taken and the other left.”
net.LUK_17_37: Then the disciples said to him, “Where, Lord?” He replied to them, “Where the dead body is, there the vultures will gather.”
net.LUK_18_1: Then Jesus told them a parable to show them they should always pray and not lose heart.
net.LUK_18_2: He said, “In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
net.LUK_18_3: There was also a widow in that city who kept coming to him and saying, ‘Give me justice against my adversary.’
net.LUK_18_4: For a while he refused, but later on he said to himself, ‘Though I neither fear God nor have regard for people,
net.LUK_18_5: yet because this widow keeps on bothering me, I will give her justice, or in the end she will wear me out by her unending pleas.’”
net.LUK_18_6: And the Lord said, “Listen to what the unrighteous judge says!
net.LUK_18_7: Won’t God give justice to his chosen ones, who cry out to him day and night? Will he delay long to help them?
net.LUK_18_8: I tell you, he will give them justice speedily. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on earth?” The Parable of the Pharisee and Tax Collector
net.LUK_18_9: Jesus also told this parable to some who were confident that they were righteous and looked down on everyone else.
net.LUK_18_10: “Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
net.LUK_18_11: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself like this: ‘God, I thank you that I am not like other people: extortionists, unrighteous people, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
net.LUK_18_12: I fast twice a week; I give a tenth of everything I get.’
net.LUK_18_13: The tax collector, however, stood far off and would not even look up to heaven, but beat his breast and said, ‘God, be merciful to me, sinner that I am!’
net.LUK_18_14: I tell you that this man went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but he who humbles himself will be exalted.” Jesus and Little Children
net.LUK_18_15: Now people were even bringing their babies to him for him to touch. But when the disciples saw it, they began to scold those who brought them.
net.LUK_18_16: But Jesus called for the children, saying, “Let the little children come to me and do not try to stop them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
net.LUK_18_17: I tell you the truth, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a child will never enter it.” The Wealthy Ruler
net.LUK_18_18: Now a certain leader asked him, “Good teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
net.LUK_18_19: Jesus said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good except God alone.
net.LUK_18_20: You know the commandments: ‘Do not commit adultery, do not murder, do not steal, do not give false testimony, honor your father and mother.’”
net.LUK_18_21: The man replied, “I have wholeheartedly obeyed all these laws since my youth.”
net.LUK_18_22: When Jesus heard this, he said to him, “One thing you still lack. Sell all that you have and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
net.LUK_18_23: But when the man heard this he became very sad, for he was extremely wealthy.
net.LUK_18_24: When Jesus noticed this, he said, “How hard it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God!
net.LUK_18_25: In fact, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.”
net.LUK_18_26: Those who heard this said, “Then who can be saved?”
net.LUK_18_27: He replied, “What is impossible for mere humans is possible for God.”
net.LUK_18_28: And Peter said, “Look, we have left everything we own to follow you!
net.LUK_18_29: Then Jesus said to them, “I tell you the truth, there is no one who has left home or wife or brothers or parents or children for the sake of God’s kingdom
net.LUK_18_30: who will not receive many times more in this age —and in the age to come, eternal life.” Another Prediction of Jesus’ Passion
net.LUK_18_31: Then Jesus took the twelve aside and said to them, “Look, we are going up to Jerusalem, and everything that is written about the Son of Man by the prophets will be accomplished.
net.LUK_18_32: For he will be handed over to the Gentiles; he will be mocked, mistreated, and spat on.
net.LUK_18_33: They will flog him severely and kill him. Yet on the third day he will rise again.”
net.LUK_18_34: But the twelve understood none of these things. This saying was hidden from them, and they did not grasp what Jesus meant. Healing a Blind Man
net.LUK_18_35: As Jesus approached Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the road begging.
net.LUK_18_36: When he heard a crowd going by, he asked what was going on.
net.LUK_18_37: They told him, “Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.”
net.LUK_18_38: So he called out, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
net.LUK_18_39: And those who were in front scolded him to get him to be quiet, but he shouted even more, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
net.LUK_18_40: So Jesus stopped and ordered the beggar to be brought to him. When the man came near, Jesus asked him,
net.LUK_18_41: “What do you want me to do for you?” He replied, “Lord, let me see again.”
net.LUK_18_42: Jesus said to him, “Receive your sight; your faith has healed you.”
net.LUK_18_43: And immediately he regained his sight and followed Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they too gave praise to God.
net.LUK_19_1: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through it.
net.LUK_19_2: Now a man named Zacchaeus was there; he was a chief tax collector and was rich.
net.LUK_19_3: He was trying to get a look at Jesus, but being a short man he could not see over the crowd.
net.LUK_19_4: So he ran on ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree to see him, because Jesus was going to pass that way.
net.LUK_19_5: And when Jesus came to that place, he looked up and said to him, “Zacchaeus, come down quickly, because I must stay at your house today.”
net.LUK_19_6: So he came down quickly and welcomed Jesus joyfully.
net.LUK_19_7: And when the people saw it, they all complained, “He has gone in to be the guest of a man who is a sinner.”
net.LUK_19_8: But Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, “Look, Lord, half of my possessions I now give to the poor, and if I have cheated anyone of anything, I am paying back four times as much!”
net.LUK_19_9: Then Jesus said to him, “Today salvation has come to this household, because he too is a son of Abraham!
net.LUK_19_10: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save the lost.” The Parable of the Ten Minas
net.LUK_19_11: While the people were listening to these things, Jesus proceeded to tell a parable, because he was near to Jerusalem, and because they thought that the kingdom of God was going to appear immediately.
net.LUK_19_12: Therefore he said, “A nobleman went to a distant country to receive for himself a kingdom and then return.
net.LUK_19_13: And he summoned ten of his slaves, gave them ten minas, and said to them, ‘Do business with these until I come back.’
net.LUK_19_14: But his citizens hated him and sent a delegation after him, saying, ‘We do not want this man to be king over us!’
net.LUK_19_15: When he returned after receiving the kingdom, he summoned these slaves to whom he had given the money. He wanted to know how much they had earned by trading.
net.LUK_19_16: So the first one came before him and said, ‘Sir, your mina has made ten minas more.’
net.LUK_19_17: And the king said to him, ‘Well done, good slave! Because you have been faithful in a very small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.’
net.LUK_19_18: Then the second one came and said, ‘Sir, your mina has made five minas.’
net.LUK_19_19: So the king said to him, ‘And you are to be over five cities.’
net.LUK_19_20: Then another slave came and said, ‘Sir, here is your mina that I put away for safekeeping in a piece of cloth.
net.LUK_19_21: For I was afraid of you, because you are a severe man. You withdraw what you did not deposit and reap what you did not sow.’
net.LUK_19_22: The king said to him, ‘I will judge you by your own words, you wicked slave! So you knew, did you, that I was a severe man, withdrawing what I didn’t deposit and reaping what I didn’t sow?
net.LUK_19_23: Why then didn’t you put my money in the bank, so that when I returned I could have collected it with interest?’
net.LUK_19_24: And he said to his attendants, ‘Take the mina from him, and give it to the one who has ten.’
net.LUK_19_25: But they said to him, ‘Sir, he has ten minas already!’
net.LUK_19_26: ‘I tell you that everyone who has will be given more, but from the one who does not have, even what he has will be taken away.
net.LUK_19_27: But as for these enemies of mine who did not want me to be their king, bring them here and slaughter them in front of me!’” The Triumphal Entry
net.LUK_19_28: After Jesus had said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
net.LUK_19_29: Now when he approached Bethphage and Bethany, at the place called the Mount of Olives, he sent two of the disciples,
net.LUK_19_30: telling them, “Go to the village ahead of you. When you enter it, you will find a colt tied there that has never been ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
net.LUK_19_31: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you untying it?’ just say, ‘The Lord needs it.’”
net.LUK_19_32: So those who were sent ahead found it exactly as he had told them.
net.LUK_19_33: As they were untying the colt, its owners asked them, “Why are you untying that colt?”
net.LUK_19_35: Then they brought it to Jesus, threw their cloaks on the colt, and had Jesus get on it.
net.LUK_19_36: As he rode along, they spread their cloaks on the road.
net.LUK_19_37: As he approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole crowd of his disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works they had seen:
net.LUK_19_38: “Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven and glory in the highest!”
net.LUK_19_39: But some of the Pharisees in the crowd said to him, “Teacher, rebuke your disciples.”
net.LUK_19_40: He answered, “I tell you, if they keep silent, the very stones will cry out!” Jesus Weeps for Jerusalem under Judgment
net.LUK_19_41: Now when Jesus approached and saw the city, he wept over it,
net.LUK_19_42: saying, “If you had only known on this day, even you, the things that make for peace! But now they are hidden from your eyes.
net.LUK_19_43: For the days will come upon you when your enemies will build an embankment against you and surround you and close in on you from every side.
net.LUK_19_44: They will demolish you —you and your children within your walls —and they will not leave within you one stone on top of another, because you did not recognize the time of your visitation from God.” Cleansing the Temple
net.LUK_19_45: Then Jesus entered the temple courts and began to drive out those who were selling things there,
net.LUK_19_46: saying to them, “It is written, ‘My house will be a house of prayer,’ but you have turned it into a den of robbers!”
net.LUK_19_47: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple courts. The chief priests and the experts in the law and the prominent leaders among the people were seeking to assassinate him,
net.LUK_19_48: but they could not find a way to do it, for all the people hung on his words.
net.LUK_20_1: Now one day, as Jesus was teaching the people in the temple courts and proclaiming the gospel, the chief priests and the experts in the law with the elders came up
net.LUK_20_2: and said to him, “Tell us: By what authority are you doing these things? Or who is it who gave you this authority?”
net.LUK_20_3: He answered them, “I will also ask you a question, and you tell me:
net.LUK_20_4: John’s baptism —was it from heaven or from people?”
net.LUK_20_5: So they discussed it with one another, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why did you not believe him?’
net.LUK_20_6: But if we say, ‘From people,’ all the people will stone us, because they are convinced that John was a prophet.”
net.LUK_20_7: So they replied that they did not know where it came from.
net.LUK_20_8: Then Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by whose authority I do these things.” The Parable of the Tenants
net.LUK_20_9: Then he began to tell the people this parable: “A man planted a vineyard, leased it to tenant farmers, and went on a journey for a long time.
net.LUK_20_10: When harvest time came, he sent a slave to the tenants so that they would give him his portion of the crop. However, the tenants beat his slave and sent him away empty-handed.
net.LUK_20_11: So he sent another slave. They beat this one too, treated him outrageously, and sent him away empty-handed.
net.LUK_20_12: So he sent still a third. They even wounded this one, and threw him out.
net.LUK_20_13: Then the owner of the vineyard said, ‘What should I do? I will send my one dear son; perhaps they will respect him.’
net.LUK_20_14: But when the tenants saw him, they said to one another, ‘This is the heir; let’s kill him so the inheritance will be ours!’
net.LUK_20_15: So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
net.LUK_20_16: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.” When the people heard this, they said, “May this never happen!”
net.LUK_20_17: But Jesus looked straight at them and said, “Then what is the meaning of that which is written: ‘The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone’?
net.LUK_20_18: Everyone who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, and the one on whom it falls will be crushed.”
net.LUK_20_19: Then the experts in the law and the chief priests wanted to arrest him that very hour, because they realized he had told this parable against them. But they were afraid of the people. Paying Taxes to Caesar
net.LUK_20_20: Then they watched him carefully and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to take advantage of what he might say so that they could deliver him up to the authority and jurisdiction of the governor.
net.LUK_20_21: Thus they asked him, “Teacher, we know that you speak and teach correctly, and show no partiality, but teach the way of God in accordance with the truth.
net.LUK_20_22: Is it right for us to pay the tribute tax to Caesar or not?”
net.LUK_20_23: But Jesus perceived their deceit and said to them,
net.LUK_20_24: “Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription are on it?” They said, “Caesar’s.”
net.LUK_20_25: So he said to them, “Then give to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
net.LUK_20_26: Thus they were unable in the presence of the people to trap him with his own words. And stunned by his answer, they fell silent. Marriage and the Resurrection
net.LUK_20_27: Now some Sadducees (who contend that there is no resurrection) came to him.
net.LUK_20_28: They asked him, “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies leaving a wife but no children, that man must marry the widow and father children for his brother.
net.LUK_20_29: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman and died without children.
net.LUK_20_31: and then the third married her, and in this same way all seven died, leaving no children.
net.LUK_20_33: In the resurrection, therefore, whose wife will the woman be? For all seven had married her.”
net.LUK_20_34: So Jesus said to them, “The people of this age marry and are given in marriage.
net.LUK_20_35: But those who are regarded as worthy to share in that age and in the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.
net.LUK_20_36: In fact, they can no longer die, because they are equal to angels and are sons of God, since they are sons of the resurrection.
net.LUK_20_37: But even Moses revealed that the dead are raised in the passage about the bush, where he calls the Lord the God of Abraham and the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob.
net.LUK_20_38: Now he is not God of the dead, but of the living, for all live before him.”
net.LUK_20_39: Then some of the experts in the law answered, “Teacher, you have spoken well!”
net.LUK_20_40: For they did not dare any longer to ask him anything. The Messiah: David’s Son and Lord
net.LUK_20_41: But he said to them, “How is it that they say that the Christ is David’s son?
net.LUK_20_42: For David himself says in the book of Psalms, ‘The Lord said to my lord, “Sit at my right hand,
net.LUK_20_43: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.”’
net.LUK_20_44: If David then calls him ‘Lord,’ how can he be his son?” Jesus Warns the Disciples against Pride
net.LUK_20_45: As all the people were listening, Jesus said to his disciples,
net.LUK_20_46: “Beware of the experts in the law. They like walking around in long robes, and they love elaborate greetings in the marketplaces and the best seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at banquets.
net.LUK_20_47: They devour widows’ property, and as a show make long prayers. They will receive a more severe punishment.”
net.LUK_21_1: Jesus looked up and saw the rich putting their gifts into the offering box.
net.LUK_21_2: He also saw a poor widow put in two small copper coins.
net.LUK_21_3: He said, “I tell you the truth, this poor widow has put in more than all of them.
net.LUK_21_4: For they all offered their gifts out of their wealth. But she, out of her poverty, put in everything she had to live on.” The Signs of the End of the Age
net.LUK_21_5: Now while some were speaking about the temple, how it was adorned with beautiful stones and offerings, Jesus said,
net.LUK_21_6: “As for these things that you are gazing at, the days will come when not one stone will be left on another. All will be torn down!”
net.LUK_21_7: So they asked him, “Teacher, when will these things happen? And what will be the sign that these things are about to take place?”
net.LUK_21_8: He said, “Watch out that you are not misled. For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he,’ and, ‘The time is near.’ Do not follow them!
net.LUK_21_9: And when you hear of wars and rebellions, do not be afraid. For these things must happen first, but the end will not come at once.” Persecution of Disciples
net.LUK_21_10: Then he said to them, “Nation will rise up in arms against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
net.LUK_21_11: There will be great earthquakes, and famines and plagues in various places, and there will be terrifying sights and great signs from heaven.
net.LUK_21_12: But before all this, they will seize you and persecute you, handing you over to the synagogues and prisons. You will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
net.LUK_21_13: This will be a time for you to serve as witnesses.
net.LUK_21_14: Therefore be resolved not to rehearse ahead of time how to make your defense.
net.LUK_21_15: For I will give you the words along with the wisdom that none of your adversaries will be able to withstand or contradict.
net.LUK_21_16: You will be betrayed even by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends, and they will have some of you put to death.
net.LUK_21_17: You will be hated by everyone because of my name.
net.LUK_21_18: Yet not a hair of your head will perish.
net.LUK_21_19: By your endurance you will gain your lives. The Desolation of Jerusalem
net.LUK_21_20: “But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation has come near.
net.LUK_21_21: Then those who are in Judea must flee to the mountains. Those who are inside the city must depart. Those who are out in the country must not enter it,
net.LUK_21_22: because these are days of vengeance, to fulfill all that is written.
net.LUK_21_23: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing their babies in those days! For there will be great distress on the earth and wrath against this people.
net.LUK_21_24: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be led away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be trampled down by the Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled. The Arrival of the Son of Man
net.LUK_21_25: “And there will be signs in the sun and moon and stars, and on the earth nations will be in distress, anxious over the roaring of the sea and the surging waves.
net.LUK_21_26: People will be fainting from fear and from the expectation of what is coming on the world, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
net.LUK_21_27: Then they will see the Son of Man arriving in a cloud with power and great glory.
net.LUK_21_28: But when these things begin to happen, stand up and raise your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.” The Parable of the Fig Tree
net.LUK_21_29: Then he told them a parable: “Look at the fig tree and all the other trees.
net.LUK_21_30: When they sprout leaves, you see for yourselves and know that summer is now near.
net.LUK_21_31: So also you, when you see these things happening, know that the kingdom of God is near.
net.LUK_21_32: I tell you the truth, this generation will not pass away until all these things take place.
net.LUK_21_33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away. Be Ready!
net.LUK_21_34: “But be on your guard so that your hearts are not weighed down with dissipation and drunkenness and the worries of this life, and that day close down upon you suddenly like a trap.
net.LUK_21_35: For it will overtake all who live on the face of the whole earth.
net.LUK_21_36: But stay alert at all times, praying that you may have strength to escape all these things that must happen, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
net.LUK_21_37: So every day Jesus was teaching in the temple courts, but at night he went and stayed on the Mount of Olives.
net.LUK_21_38: And all the people came to him early in the morning to listen to him in the temple courts.
net.LUK_22_1: Now the Feast of Unleavened Bread, which is called the Passover, was approaching.
net.LUK_22_2: The chief priests and the experts in the law were trying to find some way to execute Jesus, for they were afraid of the people.
net.LUK_22_3: Then Satan entered Judas, the one called Iscariot, who was one of the twelve.
net.LUK_22_4: He went away and discussed with the chief priests and officers of the temple guard how he might betray Jesus, handing him over to them.
net.LUK_22_5: They were delighted and arranged to give him money.
net.LUK_22_6: So Judas agreed and began looking for an opportunity to betray Jesus when no crowd was present. The Passover
net.LUK_22_7: Then the day for the feast of Unleavened Bread came, on which the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed.
net.LUK_22_8: Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare the Passover for us to eat.”
net.LUK_22_9: They said to him, “Where do you want us to prepare it?”
net.LUK_22_10: He said to them, “Listen, when you have entered the city, a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him into the house that he enters,
net.LUK_22_11: and tell the owner of the house, ‘The Teacher says to you, “Where is the guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’
net.LUK_22_12: Then he will show you a large furnished room upstairs. Make preparations there.”
net.LUK_22_13: So they went and found things just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover. The Lord’s Supper
net.LUK_22_14: Now when the hour came, Jesus took his place at the table and the apostles joined him.
net.LUK_22_15: And he said to them, “I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
net.LUK_22_16: For I tell you, I will not eat it again until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.”
net.LUK_22_17: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks he said, “Take this and divide it among yourselves.
net.LUK_22_18: For I tell you that from now on I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes.”
net.LUK_22_19: Then he took bread, and after giving thanks he broke it and gave it to them, saying, “This is my body which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
net.LUK_22_20: And in the same way he took the cup after they had eaten, saying, “This cup that is poured out for you is the new covenant in my blood. A Final Discourse
net.LUK_22_21: “But look, the hand of the one who betrays me is with me on the table.
net.LUK_22_22: For the Son of Man is to go just as it has been determined, but woe to that man by whom he is betrayed!”
net.LUK_22_23: So they began to question one another as to which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
net.LUK_22_24: A dispute also started among them over which of them was to be regarded as the greatest.
net.LUK_22_25: So Jesus said to them, “The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them, and those in authority over them are called ‘benefactors.’
net.LUK_22_26: Not so with you; instead the one who is greatest among you must become like the youngest, and the leader like the one who serves.
net.LUK_22_27: For who is greater, the one who is seated at the table, or the one who serves? Is it not the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
net.LUK_22_28: “You are the ones who have remained with me in my trials.
net.LUK_22_29: Thus I grant to you a kingdom, just as my Father granted to me,
net.LUK_22_30: that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
net.LUK_22_31: “Simon, Simon, pay attention! Satan has demanded to have you all, to sift you like wheat,
net.LUK_22_32: but I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith may not fail. When you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.”
net.LUK_22_33: But Peter said to him, “Lord, I am ready to go with you both to prison and to death!”
net.LUK_22_34: Jesus replied, “I tell you, Peter, the rooster will not crow today until you have denied three times that you know me.”
net.LUK_22_35: Then Jesus said to them, “When I sent you out with no money bag, or traveler’s bag, or sandals, you didn’t lack anything, did you?” They replied, “Nothing.”
net.LUK_22_36: He said to them, “But now, the one who has a money bag must take it, and likewise a traveler’s bag too. And the one who has no sword must sell his cloak and buy one.
net.LUK_22_37: For I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in me, ‘And he was counted with the transgressors.’ For what is written about me is being fulfilled.”
net.LUK_22_38: So they said, “Look, Lord, here are two swords.” Then he told them, “It is enough.” On the Mount of Olives
net.LUK_22_39: Then Jesus went out and made his way, as he customarily did, to the Mount of Olives, and the disciples followed him.
net.LUK_22_40: When he came to the place, he said to them, “Pray that you will not fall into temptation.”
net.LUK_22_41: He went away from them about a stone’s throw, knelt down, and prayed,
net.LUK_22_42: “Father, if you are willing, take this cup away from me. Yet not my will but yours be done.” [
net.LUK_22_43: Then an angel from heaven appeared to him and strengthened him.
net.LUK_22_44: And in his anguish he prayed more earnestly, and his sweat was like drops of blood falling to the ground.]
net.LUK_22_45: When he got up from prayer, he came to the disciples and found them sleeping, exhausted from grief.
net.LUK_22_46: So he said to them, “Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray that you will not fall into temptation!” Betrayal and Arrest
net.LUK_22_47: While he was still speaking, suddenly a crowd appeared, and the man named Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He walked up to Jesus to kiss him.
net.LUK_22_48: But Jesus said to him, “Judas, would you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?”
net.LUK_22_49: When those who were around him saw what was about to happen, they said, “Lord, should we use our swords?”
net.LUK_22_50: Then one of them struck the high priest’s slave, cutting off his right ear.
net.LUK_22_51: But Jesus said, “Enough of this!” And he touched the man’s ear and healed him.
net.LUK_22_52: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come out to get him, “Have you come out with swords and clubs like you would against an outlaw?
net.LUK_22_53: Day after day when I was with you in the temple courts, you did not arrest me. But this is your hour, and that of the power of darkness!” Jesus’ Condemnation and Peter’s Denials
net.LUK_22_54: Then they arrested Jesus, led him away, and brought him into the high priest’s house. But Peter was following at a distance.
net.LUK_22_55: When they had made a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat down among them.
net.LUK_22_56: Then a slave girl, seeing him as he sat in the firelight, stared at him and said, “This man was with him too!”
net.LUK_22_57: But Peter denied it: “Woman, I don’t know him!”
net.LUK_22_58: Then a little later someone else saw him and said, “You are one of them too.” But Peter said, “Man, I am not!”
net.LUK_22_59: And after about an hour still another insisted, “Certainly this man was with him, because he too is a Galilean.”
net.LUK_22_60: But Peter said, “Man, I don’t know what you’re talking about!” At that moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
net.LUK_22_61: Then the Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said to him, “Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.”
net.LUK_22_62: And he went outside and wept bitterly.
net.LUK_22_63: Now the men who were holding Jesus under guard began to mock him and beat him.
net.LUK_22_64: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, “Prophesy! Who hit you?”
net.LUK_22_65: They also said many other things against him, reviling him.
net.LUK_22_66: When day came, the council of the elders of the people gathered together, both the chief priests and the experts in the law. Then they led Jesus away to their council
net.LUK_22_67: and said, “If you are the Christ, tell us.” But he said to them, “If I tell you, you will not believe,
net.LUK_22_68: and if I ask you, you will not answer.
net.LUK_22_69: But from now on the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”
net.LUK_22_70: So they all said, “Are you the Son of God, then?” He answered them, “You say that I am.”
net.LUK_22_71: Then they said, “Why do we need further testimony? We have heard it ourselves from his own lips!”
net.LUK_23_1: Then the whole group of them rose up and brought Jesus before Pilate.
net.LUK_23_2: They began to accuse him, saying, “We found this man subverting our nation, forbidding us to pay the tribute tax to Caesar and claiming that he himself is Christ, a king.”
net.LUK_23_3: So Pilate asked Jesus, “Are you the king of the Jews?” He replied, “You say so.”
net.LUK_23_4: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, “I find no basis for an accusation against this man.”
net.LUK_23_5: But they persisted in saying, “He incites the people by teaching throughout all Judea. It started in Galilee and ended up here!” Jesus Brought Before Herod
net.LUK_23_6: Now when Pilate heard this, he asked whether the man was a Galilean.
net.LUK_23_7: When he learned that he was from Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him over to Herod, who also happened to be in Jerusalem at that time.
net.LUK_23_8: When Herod saw Jesus, he was very glad, for he had long desired to see him, because he had heard about him and was hoping to see him perform some miraculous sign.
net.LUK_23_9: So Herod questioned him at considerable length; Jesus gave him no answer.
net.LUK_23_10: The chief priests and the experts in the law were there, vehemently accusing him.
net.LUK_23_11: Even Herod with his soldiers treated him with contempt and mocked him. Then, dressing him in elegant clothes, Herod sent him back to Pilate.
net.LUK_23_12: That very day Herod and Pilate became friends with each other, for prior to this they had been enemies. Jesus Brought Before the Crowd
net.LUK_23_13: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the leaders, and the people,
net.LUK_23_14: and said to them, “You brought me this man as one who was misleading the people. When I examined him before you, I did not find this man guilty of anything you accused him of doing.
net.LUK_23_15: Neither did Herod, for he sent him back to us. Look, he has done nothing deserving death.
net.LUK_23_16: I will therefore have him flogged and release him.”
net.LUK_23_18: But they all shouted out together, “Take this man away! Release Barabbas for us!”
net.LUK_23_19: (This was a man who had been thrown into prison for an insurrection started in the city, and for murder.)
net.LUK_23_20: Pilate addressed them once again because he wanted to release Jesus.
net.LUK_23_21: But they kept on shouting, “Crucify, crucify him!”
net.LUK_23_22: A third time he said to them, “Why? What wrong has he done? I have found him guilty of no crime deserving death. I will therefore flog him and release him.”
net.LUK_23_23: But they were insistent, demanding with loud shouts that he be crucified. And their shouts prevailed.
net.LUK_23_24: So Pilate decided that their demand should be granted.
net.LUK_23_25: He released the man they asked for, who had been thrown in prison for insurrection and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will. The Crucifixion
net.LUK_23_26: As they led him away, they seized Simon of Cyrene, who was coming in from the country. They placed the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
net.LUK_23_27: A great number of the people followed him, among them women who were mourning and wailing for him.
net.LUK_23_28: But Jesus turned to them and said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.
net.LUK_23_29: For this is certain: The days are coming when they will say, ‘Blessed are the barren, the wombs that never bore children, and the breasts that never nursed!’
net.LUK_23_30: Then they will begin to say to the mountains, ‘Fall on us!’ and to the hills, ‘Cover us!’
net.LUK_23_31: For if such things are done when the wood is green, what will happen when it is dry?”
net.LUK_23_32: Two other criminals were also led away to be executed with him.
net.LUK_23_33: So when they came to the place that is called “The Skull,” they crucified him there, along with the criminals, one on his right and one on his left.
net.LUK_23_34: [But Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they don’t know what they are doing.”] Then they threw dice to divide his clothes.
net.LUK_23_35: The people also stood there watching, but the leaders ridiculed him, saying, “He saved others. Let him save himself if he is the Christ of God, his chosen one!”
net.LUK_23_36: The soldiers also mocked him, coming up and offering him sour wine,
net.LUK_23_37: and saying, “If you are the king of the Jews, save yourself!”
net.LUK_23_38: There was also an inscription over him, “This is the king of the Jews.”
net.LUK_23_39: One of the criminals who was hanging there railed at him, saying, “Aren’t you the Christ? Save yourself and us!”
net.LUK_23_40: But the other rebuked him, saying, “Don’t you fear God, since you are under the same sentence of condemnation?
net.LUK_23_41: And we rightly so, for we are getting what we deserve for what we did, but this man has done nothing wrong.”
net.LUK_23_42: Then he said, “Jesus, remember me when you come in your kingdom.”
net.LUK_23_43: And Jesus said to him, “I tell you the truth, today you will be with me in paradise.”
net.LUK_23_44: It was now about noon, and darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon,
net.LUK_23_45: because the sun’s light failed. The temple curtain was torn in two.
net.LUK_23_46: Then Jesus, calling out with a loud voice, said, “Father, into your hands I commit my spirit!” And after he said this he breathed his last.
net.LUK_23_47: Now when the centurion saw what had happened, he praised God and said, “Certainly this man was innocent!”
net.LUK_23_48: And all the crowds that had assembled for this spectacle, when they saw what had taken place, returned home beating their breasts.
net.LUK_23_49: And all those who knew Jesus stood at a distance, and the women who had followed him from Galilee saw these things. Jesus’ Burial
net.LUK_23_50: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council, a good and righteous man.
net.LUK_23_51: (He had not consented to their plan and action.) He was from the Judean town of Arimathea, and was looking forward to the kingdom of God.
net.LUK_23_52: He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
net.LUK_23_53: Then he took it down, wrapped it in a linen cloth, and placed it in a tomb cut out of the rock, where no one had yet been buried.
net.LUK_23_54: It was the day of preparation and the Sabbath was beginning.
net.LUK_23_55: The women who had accompanied Jesus from Galilee followed, and they saw the tomb and how his body was laid in it.
net.LUK_23_56: Then they returned and prepared aromatic spices and perfumes. On the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.
net.LUK_24_1: Now on the first day of the week, at early dawn, the women went to the tomb, taking the aromatic spices they had prepared.
net.LUK_24_2: They found that the stone had been rolled away from the tomb,
net.LUK_24_3: but when they went in, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
net.LUK_24_4: While they were perplexed about this, suddenly two men stood beside them in dazzling attire.
net.LUK_24_5: The women were terribly frightened and bowed their faces to the ground, but the men said to them, “Why do you look for the living among the dead?
net.LUK_24_6: He is not here, but has been raised! Remember how he told you, while he was still in Galilee,
net.LUK_24_7: that the Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and on the third day rise again.”
net.LUK_24_8: Then the women remembered his words,
net.LUK_24_9: and when they returned from the tomb they told all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.
net.LUK_24_10: Now it was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
net.LUK_24_11: But these words seemed like pure nonsense to them, and they did not believe them.
net.LUK_24_12: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb. He bent down and saw only the strips of linen cloth; then he went home, wondering what had happened. Jesus Walks the Road to Emmaus
net.LUK_24_13: Now that very day two of them were on their way to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
net.LUK_24_14: They were talking to each other about all the things that had happened.
net.LUK_24_15: While they were talking and debating these things, Jesus himself approached and began to accompany them
net.LUK_24_16: (but their eyes were kept from recognizing him).
net.LUK_24_17: Then he said to them, “What are these matters you are discussing so intently as you walk along?” And they stood still, looking sad.
net.LUK_24_18: Then one of them, named Cleopas, answered him, “Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who doesn’t know the things that have happened there in these days?”
net.LUK_24_19: He said to them, “What things?” “The things concerning Jesus the Nazarene,” they replied, “a man who, with his powerful deeds and words, proved to be a prophet before God and all the people;
net.LUK_24_20: and how our chief priests and leaders handed him over to be condemned to death, and crucified him.
net.LUK_24_21: But we had hoped that he was the one who was going to redeem Israel. Not only this, but it is now the third day since these things happened.
net.LUK_24_22: Furthermore, some women of our group amazed us. They were at the tomb early this morning,
net.LUK_24_23: and when they did not find his body, they came back and said they had seen a vision of angels, who said he was alive.
net.LUK_24_24: Then some of those who were with us went to the tomb, and found it just as the women had said, but they did not see him.”
net.LUK_24_25: So he said to them, “You foolish people —how slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken!
net.LUK_24_26: Wasn’t it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and enter into his glory?”
net.LUK_24_27: Then beginning with Moses and all the prophets, he interpreted to them the things written about himself in all the scriptures.
net.LUK_24_28: So they approached the village where they were going. He acted as though he wanted to go farther,
net.LUK_24_29: but they urged him, “Stay with us, because it is getting toward evening and the day is almost done.” So he went in to stay with them.
net.LUK_24_30: When he had taken his place at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
net.LUK_24_31: At this point their eyes were opened and they recognized him. Then he vanished out of their sight.
net.LUK_24_32: They said to each other, “Didn’t our hearts burn within us while he was speaking with us on the road, while he was explaining the scriptures to us?”
net.LUK_24_33: So they got up that very hour and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and those with them gathered together
net.LUK_24_34: and saying, “The Lord has really risen, and has appeared to Simon!”
net.LUK_24_35: Then they told what had happened on the road, and how they recognized him when he broke the bread. Jesus Makes a Final Appearance
net.LUK_24_36: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
net.LUK_24_37: But they were startled and terrified, thinking they saw a ghost.
net.LUK_24_38: Then he said to them, “Why are you frightened, and why do doubts arise in your hearts?
net.LUK_24_39: Look at my hands and my feet; it’s me! Touch me and see; a ghost does not have flesh and bones like you see I have.”
net.LUK_24_40: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
net.LUK_24_41: And while they still could not believe it (because of their joy) and were amazed, he said to them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”
net.LUK_24_42: So they gave him a piece of broiled fish,
net.LUK_24_43: and he took it and ate it in front of them. Jesus’ Final Commission
net.LUK_24_44: Then he said to them, “These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you, that everything written about me in the law of Moses and the prophets and the psalms must be fulfilled.”
net.LUK_24_45: Then he opened their minds so they could understand the scriptures,
net.LUK_24_46: and said to them, “Thus it stands written that the Christ would suffer and would rise from the dead on the third day,
net.LUK_24_47: and repentance for the forgiveness of sins would be proclaimed in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
net.LUK_24_48: You are witnesses of these things.
net.LUK_24_49: And look, I am sending you what my Father promised. But stay in the city until you have been clothed with power from on high.” Jesus’ Departure
net.LUK_24_50: Then Jesus led them out as far as Bethany, and lifting up his hands, he blessed them.
net.LUK_24_51: Now during the blessing he departed and was taken up into heaven.
net.LUK_24_52: So they worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,
net.LUK_24_53: and were continually in the temple courts blessing God.
